code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test15-DEC-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and mild hyperchloremia. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), indicating possible renal and/or hepatic dysfunction. Hemoglobin A1c is low, which may reflect anemia or recent hypoglycemia. Recommend further evaluation for renal and hepatic pathology and correlation with clinical findings.
